mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0%, labor market deterioration signals severe demand contraction, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances when UNRATE > 8.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and capital expenditure recovery, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deltas in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation regime where CPIAUCSL exceeds 230, persistent price pressures force monetary tightening expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are not significantly negative when CPIAUCSL > 230 across multiple 1-year test windows.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect expanding corporate output capacity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show zero or negative correlation with INDPRO growth over a 24-month rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll employment gain exceeding 150,000 signals robust labor demand and wage growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by > 150.0 in a single month
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative in >50% of months following PAYEMS gains > 150.0.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth decelerates while consumer prices accelerate, stagflationary pressures compress corporate margins and equity valuations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint condition of falling GDPC1 growth and rising CPIAUCSL fails to predict negative 60-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a low-unemployment regime below 7.5%, rising industrial production confirms capacity utilization improvements, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO trends during UNRATE < 7.5% do not yield positive 20-day forward returns in >60% of observed cases.
